name: Belorado Castle and Camino Francés Trail
name_original: Camino Redoña y Castillo de Belorado — circular desde Belorado
description: Hike the easy 3.7-mile Belorado Castle and Camino Francés Trail for panoramic views from the castle ruins and a taste of the famous pilgrima
region_intro: Hiking trails around Belorado offer a diverse landscape, characterized by rolling hills, historical paths, and natural woodlands. The region features varied terrain, including sections of the Camino Francés, providing both cultural and natural exploration opportunities. Belorado's surroundings are suitable for different hiking preferences, from gentle walks to more challenging ascents. The area provides a blend of open country and shaded routes, making it appealing for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Belorado The most popular hiking route is Alto Oca Trail (PR-BU 55), a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain and offers views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Belorado Castle and Camino Francés Trail, an easy 3.7 miles (5.9 km) path. This route combines historical landmarks with sections of the famous pilgrimage trail. Local hikers also love the To Espinosa – Plaza Mayor de Belorado loop from Belorado, a 15.7 miles (25.3 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and villages, often completed in about 6 hours 44 minutes. Hiking around Belorado is defined by historical routes, rolling hills, and natural woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails. The routes in Belorado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 350 reviews. More than 1900 hikers have used komoot to explore Belorado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Belorado?
A: Yes, Belorado offers several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045463962/belorado-castle-and-camino-frances-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belorado Castle and Camino Francés Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.7-mile (5.9 km) path that combines historical landmarks with sections of the famous pilgrimage route. Overall, there are 21 easy routes available in the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Belorado?
A: Hiking around Belorado is characterized by diverse terrain, including rolling hills, historical paths, and natural woodlands. You'll find a blend of open country and shaded routes, with options ranging from gentle walks to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options around Belorado?
A: Absolutely. Many of the easier trails are suitable for families. The gentle slopes and historical points of interest, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045463962/belorado-castle-and-camino-frances-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belorado Castle and Camino Francés Trail&lt;/a&gt;, make for an enjoyable outing with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Belorado?
A: Most trails in the Belorado region are dog-friendly, especially those in rural and natural areas. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near livestock or in populated areas, and to clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Belorado?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;To Espinosa – Plaza Mayor de Belorado loop from Belorado&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 15.7-mile (25.3 km) trail through rural landscapes. For a shorter option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40312663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Fresneña&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.1 miles (8.3 km).

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural features to see while hiking in Belorado?
A: Belorado's surroundings offer several points of interest. You can explore peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5278503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz De Guirnaldo&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3682734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of San Lorenzo from Masoga&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4705206" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock formations on the banks of the Tirón River&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5550560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arboleda del Sur – Horse Chestnut Trees of Ezcaray&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Belorado?
A: The hiking routes in Belorado are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 350 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the historical significance of routes like the Camino Francés, and the variety of trails suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trailheads in Belorado?
A: While Belorado is a smaller town, public transport options to specific trailheads outside the town center may be limited. Many routes, especially those starting directly from Belorado, are easily accessible on foot from the town itself. For trails further afield, a car is generally recommended.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Belorado?
A: In Belorado, you can typically find parking in and around the town center. For specific trailheads outside the main town, parking is often available in designated areas or small village squares. It's advisable to check local signage for any restrictions.

Q: Are there any cafes or facilities near the hiking trails in Belorado?
A: Belorado, being a stop on the Camino Francés, has several cafes, restaurants, and shops in the town center where you can find refreshments and facilities. Along some longer routes, you might pass through smaller villages that also offer basic amenities. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3266634" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente La Teja&lt;/a&gt; is a facility highlight.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Belorado?
A: The spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) months generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions in Belorado, with mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be warm, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for hiking, especially on lower elevation trails.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in Belorado?
A: There are over 130 hiking trails available around Belorado, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and skill levels. This includes 21 easy, 89 moderate, and 24 difficult routes.
tips: Leaving the town of Belorado, the Camino de Redoña appears and where, in addition, it is where the Camino de Santiago passes that goes in an easterly direction.

On the hill that is on the east side of the town of Belorado there are the remains of what used to be the castle, which apart from seeing the constructions, you can see beautiful views of the town that is just below.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Belorado Castle and Camino Francés Trail?
faqs.answer: This specific trail is designed as an easy hike, typically taking around 1 hour and 40 minutes to complete. It covers a distance of approximately 5.9 kilometers (3.7 miles).

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The Belorado Castle and Camino Francés Trail is rated as easy. It involves a moderate elevation gain of about 104 meters (340 feet), making it suitable for most fitness levels, including beginners and families.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a blend of historical sites and scenic views. You'll walk along a section of the Camino de Santiago in Belorado (Redoña trail), visit the historic Ruins of Belorado Castle with panoramic views, and pass through the charming Plaza Mayor de Belorado.

faqs.question: Is the Belorado Castle and Camino Francés Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. Dogs are welcome, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through town areas or near livestock, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when the weather is mild and pleasant. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures and potential for rain or snow, making some sections muddy.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Belorado is a town with various parking options. You can usually find parking near the town center or residential areas, which are close to the starting points for routes that include the castle and the Camino Francés section. Look for public parking spaces within Belorado.

faqs.question: Are there any public transport options to reach Belorado?
faqs.answer: Yes, Belorado is a well-known stop on the Camino Francés, so it's accessible by bus services connecting it to larger cities like Burgos or Logroño. This makes it convenient for hikers to reach the starting point of the trail.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Belorado Castle and Camino Francés Trail. Access to the castle ruins and the Camino Francés section is free.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a mix of surfaces. You'll encounter relatively easy and flat paths, typical of the Camino Francés, as well as some ascents to the castle ruins. Expect dirt tracks, gravel footpaths, and some paved sections as you navigate through the town and surrounding areas.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route prominently features a section of the Camino de Santiago in Belorado (Redoña trail). It also utilizes parts of the broader Camino Francés - 03 Logroño a Burgos, Camino de Santiago, and Camino Redoña.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink in Belorado after the hike?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! Belorado is a lively town with a history tied to the Camino. You'll find several cafes, restaurants, and bars, especially around the Plaza Mayor de Belorado, where you can enjoy a meal or a refreshing drink after your hike.

waypoints.name: Camino de Santiago in Belorado (Redoña trail)
